items.title: Officer who laughed at prison death among six sanctioned by WA Police

children.content: Six WA Police officers have been sanctioned over their treatment of a man in their custody who later died in prison, after the ABC revealed a constable dismissed his threat of suicide, and laughed about his death.

gallery.altText: A timelapse photo at twilight shows blurred lights outside a prison fence with three tall exterior lights

items.title: Rare high-end perfume ingredient ambergris washes up on WA beach

children.content: A highly prized, rare ingredient used in the production of luxury perfumes washes up on a Denmark beach — and it comes from the intestine of sperm whales.

image.altText: Bald man holding black and brown circular object size of a coconut

items.title: Renewables and batteries drive down fossil fuel use despite record electricity demand

children.content: A hot summer saw Australians use record amounts of electricity, but the growing share of renewable energy continued to push fossil fuels out of the grid, driving gas generation to its lowest level in 25 years.

image.altText: Silhouette of transmission lines at dusk with sun flaring through

items.title: 'No offence to Tassie': The infamous Sirengate mess 20 years on

children.content: Two decades ago, the Dockers and Saints met at York Park in Launceston. The siren sounded but the match continued — and the dreaded word "gate" was added to another footy scandal.

gallery.altText: Dockers players celebrate prematurely at York Park
A Fremantle AFL player holds his coach back as the St Kilda captain confrtons him and a Saints player points in the background.

items.title: Docker O'Driscoll reveals he contemplated suicide in emotional post

children.content: Fremantle Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll reveals his mental health struggles in an emotional social media post, amid a push from players for the AFL to implement a mental health round.

image.altText: Nathan handballing a football

items.title: Longest-serving WA MP, war hero Bill Grayden, dies at 105

children.content: WA's longest-serving parliamentarian, war hero Bill Grayden, is being remembered for his service to his country and community.

image.altText: Kokoda campaign veteran Bill Grayden at a service in Kings Park.

items.title: Shock closure of 12 WA childcare centres

children.content: Australia's largest childcare operator G8 Education will shut 40 of its centres - 12 of those are in WA.
Read the full list of local closures and hear from the union representing childcare workers

items.title: Tim Picton's accused killer pleads not guilty to manslaughter

children.content: The man accused of killing former Labor identity Tim Picton outside a Northbridge venue has pleaded not guilty to his manslaughter.

image.altText: Brodie Jake Dewar leaves court flanked by a young woman.

items.title: More women come forward after sepsis case linked to pedicure

children.content: More Perth women share their experience of needing surgery after having an adverse reaction to nail salon services, triggering a warning from the consumer watchdog.

items.title: WA farmers seeding their 'riskiest crop in years' welcome early rain

children.content: Widespread rain delivers timely relief to grain growers across the southern Wheatbelt, as they balance seeding decisions against soaring diesel and fertiliser costs.

image.altText: Man checks his rain gauge

items.title: New course helps teens navigate unique outback driving hazards

children.content: A new defensive driving course being offered in Kalgoorlie aims to equip young motorists with the skills to cope with the unique challenges of outback driving.

image.altText: A teenage boy behind the wheel of a car next to an instructor in a driver trainer course.

items.title: Experts offer theories on why WA is 'wild west' for ADHD prescriptions

children.content: With WA home to seven out of Australia's top-10 highest ADHD prescribing neighbourhoods, experts, psychiatrists and doctors weigh in with their theories.

items.title: First report from antisemitism, Bondi royal commission to be released

children.content: The Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion will deliver its first report, two months after it began and less than six months after the Bondi Beach terror attack.

image.altText: Floral tributes at Bondi Beach on day seven after terror attack

items.title: Pauline Hanson celebrates 'sexy' $1m plane gifted by Gina Rinehart

children.content: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson banks more than $3 million worth of donations, including a brand-new plane gifted by Australia's richest person, Gina Rinehart.

image.altText: A woman wearing whtie pants and red and white shirts steps onto the wing of a grey plane.

items.title: Ultra-swimmer sets world record with 55km solo feat in crocodile-filled river

children.content: Andy Donaldson took less than 12 hours to swim the challenging 55-kilometre course down the Upper Ord River in Western Australia's north.

items.title: 'It's a safety issue': Construction industry faces gambling crisis

children.content: New research reveals 89 per cent of construction workers have gambled in the past year — higher than the national average.

items.title: 'Only a little baby': Family of missing 5yo Sharon Granites speaks

children.content: Family members of a missing Alice Springs five-year-old have spoken to the ABC for the first time, pleading for anyone with information that could help find their "energetic" little girl to come forward.

items.title: Monitor appointed to address tensions between councillors at Perth shire

children.content: A fifth local authority in WA has been assigned an independent monitor, this time at the council's request, to focus on council member conduct and improving relationships.

image.altText: Row of six men in dark blue suits with one woman in a red pantsuit.

items.title: Insurers among group proposing cheaper premiums for disaster-resistant homes

children.content: As about 1.6 million households experience insurance unaffordability stress, a disparate alliance of stakeholders proposes cheaper premiums for home owners who upgrade their properties to withstand natural disasters.

image.altText: New houses sit on a hillside in a housing estate at Albion Park.

items.title: Woman and child allegedly carjacked after burning bike blocks road

children.content: Police allege Spencer Thorne held a woman and her two-year-old child hostage after carjacking them in Dalyellup, south of Bunbury, in WA's South West.

children.content: A man has been arrested after two men were stabbed in London in an area with a large Jewish population.

items.title: Just one rental in Australia affordable for JobSeekers like Jess

children.content: Anglicare Australia's latest look at rentals shows the private market effectively does not exist for poorer Australians.

items.title: Does the gas industry pay its fair share of tax?

children.content: Advocates are proposing a flat 25 per cent tax on gas exports, but representatives say the industry is being unfairly targeted.

children.content: Bunbury fisherman Mason Jones, who led a protest against the WA government's demersal fishing ban, says he is willing to go to jail to keep campaigning against the measures.

items.title: AFL pushes ahead with draft changes and not everyone is happy

children.content: The AFL is pushing ahead with draft and player-movement changes for this off-season, with Carlton president Rob Priestly and Western Bulldogs coach Luke Beveridge criticising the move.

items.title: Tigers, Eagles or Cockle Divers — what makes up a football club's name?

children.content: How have Australia's football clubs come to be named, and what do those names represent? We analysed over 1,500 senior Aussie Rules clubs to find the patterns in how clubs have named themselves.

items.title: Perth man pleads not guilty to father's murder on grounds of insanity

children.content: David Moussa, 32, pleads not guilty on grounds of insanity to murdering his father in the Perth suburb of Clarkson.

image.altText: Police tape cordons off a street with police vans parked on either side of the road and an officer walking across the street.

items.title: 'Simply not believable': Housing plan gains criticism

children.content: The West Australian government has announced it plans to build 500 homes for public servants in regional areas over five years despite a "critical worker shortage".

image.altText: A small bobcat in front of a construction site of a modular house

items.title: Police honour Kalgoorlie detectives brutally killed 100 years ago

children.content: On this day in 1926, Detective Inspector John Walsh and Detective Sergeant Alexander Pitman were shot while trying to apprehend gold thieves near Kalgoorlie. Their charred and dismembered bodies were later found at the bottom of a mine shaft.

gallery.altText: An historic black and white image of two police detectives in 1920.
An historic black and white image of police investigating a murder case.
A man and a police officer holding a wreath at a memorial service.

An historic black and white image of police investigating a murder case.
A man and a police officer holding a wreath at a memorial service.
